title: The Digital Mirror: How AI and New Drugs Are Changing Beauty Standards
subtitle: From AI-generated faces to the effects of weight-loss medication, modern technology is reshaping what we consider attractive.

summary.en: Artificial intelligence is changing how people view their own faces, leading to unrealistic expectations for plastic surgery. Patients now frequently bring AI-altered photos to surgeons, hoping to achieve perfect symmetry. Meanwhile, the rise of weight-loss drugs like Wegovy is creating a new look known as 'GLP-1 face'. Experts believe these trends are influencing both medical practice and artistic representations of beauty. Ultimately, surgeons and researchers warn that these digital and medical ideals often ignore the realities of human anatomy and health.
summary.zh: 人工智慧正在改變人們看待自己臉孔的方式，導致對整形手術產生不切實際的期望。患者現在經常帶著經人工智慧修改的照片諮詢醫師，希望能達到完美的對稱感。同時，像 Wegovy 這類減肥藥物的興起，創造了一種被稱為「GLP-1 臉」的新外貌。專家認為，這些趨勢正在影響醫療實務與藝術對美的呈現。最終，外科醫師與研究人員警告，這些數位與醫療上的理想，往往忽略了人體解剖構造與健康的現實。

paragraphs.en: In recent years, the way we think about beauty has been transformed by new technology. From artificial intelligence tools that can change a person's face in seconds to powerful new weight-loss drugs, these developments are changing how people see themselves and what they ask for from medical professionals. Plastic surgeons are reporting a significant rise in patients bringing AI-generated images to their appointments. These clients often want to look exactly like the digital versions of themselves, which feature flawless skin, sharp cheekbones, and perfect symmetry.
paragraphs.zh: 近年來，新科技徹底改變了我們對美的看法。從能在幾秒鐘內改變人臉的人工智慧工具，到強效的新型減肥藥物，這些發展正在改變人們看待自己的方式，以及他們對醫療專業人員提出的要求。整形外科醫師指出，越來越多患者帶著人工智慧生成的照片前來諮詢。這些客戶通常希望外貌能與數位版本的自己完全一致，擁有無瑕的肌膚、立體的顴骨以及完美的對稱感。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Nora Nugent, a cosmetic surgeon and president of the British Association of Aesthetic Plastic Surgeons, notes that these expectations are often impossible to meet. According to The Guardian, Dr. Nugent explains that while AI can change every pixel of a photo, human surgery does not work at such a detailed level. Surgeons must constantly remind patients that they cannot change basic human anatomy, such as the position of the eyes or the structure of the bone. Dr. Julian de Silva, a surgeon based in London, agrees, noting that AI often creates 'hyper-symmetry' that is simply not possible to recreate in real life. When patients see these perfect digital images, the expectations become 'seared' into their minds, making it difficult for them to accept the natural results of surgery.
paragraphs.zh: 身兼美容外科醫師與英國美容整形外科醫師協會主席的諾拉·紐金特醫師（Dr. Nora Nugent）指出，這些期望往往是不可能實現的。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，紐金特醫師解釋說，雖然人工智慧可以改變照片中的每一個像素，但人類外科手術無法達到如此細緻的程度。醫師必須不斷提醒患者，他們無法改變人體的基本構造，例如眼睛的位置或骨骼結構。倫敦的外科醫師朱利安·德席爾瓦醫師（Dr. Julian de Silva）也表示認同，並指出人工智慧經常創造出在現實生活中根本無法重現的「超對稱」效果。當患者看到這些完美的數位影像時，這些期望便會「烙印」在他們腦海中，使他們難以接受手術後的自然結果。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the digital world, medical treatments are also shifting beauty standards. The widespread use of weight-loss drugs, such as Wegovy and Mounjaro, has led to a phenomenon some call 'GLP-1 face'. This look often features a thinner, more hollowed-out appearance due to rapid fat loss in the cheeks and under-eye areas. While some people view this look negatively, experts suggest it may soon become a new cultural ideal. Prof. Rosalind Gill of Goldsmiths, University of London, notes that society has a history of changing its beauty standards based on available trends and consumer culture. Just as 'heroin chic' was a trend in the 1990s, the 'GLP-1 look' could become the next standard that brands and artists promote.
paragraphs.zh: 除了數位世界之外，醫療處置也在改變審美標準。減肥藥物如「Wegovy」與「Mounjaro」的廣泛使用，導致了一種被稱為「GLP-1 臉」的現象。這種外貌通常因臉頰與眼下區域的脂肪快速流失，而顯得更消瘦、更凹陷。儘管有些人對這種外貌持負面看法，但專家認為它可能很快會成為新的文化理想。倫敦大學金匠學院（Goldsmiths, University of London）的羅莎琳德·吉爾教授（Prof. Rosalind Gill）指出，社會一直有根據當前趨勢與消費文化改變審美標準的歷史。正如 1990 年代流行的「海洛因時尚」（heroin chic），「GLP-1 臉」也可能成為品牌與藝術家推崇的下一個標準。

paragraphs.en: This shift is also being noticed in the world of art. Dr. Michael Yafi, a paediatric endocrinologist at the University of Texas, Houston, argues that art has always reflected how society views health and body weight. Historically, being overweight was often a sign of wealth and status, and many famous painters portrayed heavier figures as beautiful. However, as scientific understanding of health changed in the 20th century, the ideal shifted toward thinness. Dr. Yafi believes that as more people use weight-loss medications, artists will likely begin to paint and photograph this new 'GLP-1 face'. He suggests that understanding these historical shifts can help doctors be more non-judgmental and provide better care to their patients.
paragraphs.zh: 這種轉變在藝術界也受到關注。德州大學休士頓分校（University of Texas, Houston）的小兒內分泌科醫師麥可·亞菲醫師（Dr. Michael Yafi）認為，藝術一向反映了社會對健康與體重的看法。歷史上，過重往往是財富與地位的象徵，許多著名畫家都將體態豐腴的人物描繪得十分美麗。然而，隨著 20 世紀科學對健康認知的改變，理想體態轉向了纖瘦。亞菲醫師認為，隨著越來越多人使用減肥藥物，藝術家很可能會開始繪製與拍攝這種新的「GLP-1 臉」。他建議，理解這些歷史轉變有助於醫師以更客觀、不帶偏見的態度，為患者提供更好的照護。

paragraphs.en: For surgeons, the challenge is managing the gap between digital fantasy and physical reality. Dr. Alex Karidis, a surgeon in west London, observes that when patients research procedures, they often fixate on the images they have found online and ignore the warnings from professionals. Surgeons emphasize that cosmetic surgery outcomes are not guaranteed. Factors like how a person ages, their natural healing process, and their unique anatomy mean that no two results are the same. Dr. Nugent stresses that patients must understand that there are limits to what surgery can achieve. Neither the doctor nor the patient can control everything.
paragraphs.zh: 對外科醫師而言，挑戰在於如何管理數位幻想與生理現實之間的落差。倫敦西區的外科醫師亞歷克斯·卡里迪斯醫師（Dr. Alex Karidis）觀察到，患者在研究手術時，往往過度執著於在網路上找到的影像，而忽略了專業人員的警告。外科醫師強調，美容手術的結果無法保證。諸如個人的老化方式、自然的癒合過程以及獨特的解剖構造等因素，意味著沒有兩次手術的結果會完全相同。紐金特醫師強調，患者必須理解手術能達到的效果是有極限的。醫師與患者都無法掌控一切。

paragraphs.en: As these technologies continue to develop, the influence of AI and medicine on our self-image will likely grow. While AI can offer a vision of a 'perfect' face, it remains a digital creation that does not account for the complexities of the human body. Similarly, while weight-loss drugs change physical appearance, they also reflect broader societal trends that move in and out of fashion. For now, experts advise that both patients and the public should remain critical of the images they see online. Understanding that these 'perfect' looks are often the result of software or specific medical conditions is an important step in maintaining a healthy perspective on beauty in the modern age.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著這些技術持續發展，人工智慧與醫學對我們自我形象的影響可能會日益增強。雖然人工智慧能提供一種「完美」臉孔的願景，但它終究是數位產物，並未考量人體的複雜性。同樣地，雖然減肥藥物改變了外貌，但也反映了隨流行趨勢更迭的廣泛社會現象。目前，專家建議患者與大眾應對在網路上看到的影像保持批判性思考。理解這些「完美」外貌往往是軟體運算或特定醫療狀況的結果，是現代社會維持健康審美觀的重要一步。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To burn or fix something firmly into the mind so it is difficult to forget.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 烙印；深深地刻在（腦海中）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 原意指高溫灼燒，這裡引申為記憶深刻。文中：When patients see these perfect digital images, the expectations become 'seared' into their minds, making it difficult for them to accept the natural results of surgery.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To think about something to an excessive or unhealthy degree.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常搭配介系詞 on 使用，表示對某事過度關注。文中：Dr. Alex Karidis, a surgeon in west London, observes that when patients research procedures, they often fixate on the images they have found online and ignore the warnings from professionals.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To consider or include something when making a decision or judgment.
keyTerms.noteZh: 容易誤以為是「解釋」或「佔比例」，這裡指將複雜性納入考量。文中：While AI can offer a vision of a 'perfect' face, it remains a digital creation that does not account for the complexities of the human body.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: ‘You can’t control everything’: the rise in plastic surgeons asked to create ‘AI face’

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: From high BMI to the ‘GLP-1 look’: how weight-loss jabs are changing the face of beauty

quiz.question: According to the article, why do plastic surgeons find it difficult to meet the expectations of patients who bring in AI-generated images?

options.A: Surgeons lack the necessary software to analyze the AI-generated photos.
options.B: Human anatomy imposes physical limits that cannot be altered to match digital perfection.
options.C: Patients often refuse to pay for the expensive procedures required to achieve symmetry.
options.D: AI images are based on outdated beauty standards that surgeons no longer follow.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，Dr. Nora Nugent 與 Dr. Julian de Silva 明確指出，AI 可以改變照片的每一個像素，但外科手術無法改變人類的基本解剖結構（如骨骼結構或眼睛位置），且 AI 創造的『超對稱』在現實中難以重現。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 C 關於費用並非文中提到的困難點；選項 D 錯誤，因為 AI 呈現的是當代追求的完美形象，而非過時標準。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between medical trends and societal beauty standards?

options.A: Societal beauty standards are primarily driven by the personal preferences of plastic surgeons.
options.B: The 'GLP-1 face' is a permanent change that will eventually eliminate all other beauty ideals.
options.C: Beauty standards are fluid and often reflect the medical or technological capabilities of the time.
options.D: Patients who use weight-loss drugs are generally more satisfied with their appearance than those who do not.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。綜合文章第三段與第四段，作者提到美感標準隨著時代與科技（如減肥藥、藝術史）而不斷演變。Prof. Rosalind Gill 指出社會美感標準隨趨勢改變，而 Dr. Yafi 則提到藝術如何反映當時對健康與體態的理解。選項 A 與文意不符；選項 B 將『趨勢』誤解為『永久性』；選項 D 在文中並未討論使用者的滿意度比較。

quiz.question: In the second paragraph, the author writes that expectations become 'seared' into patients' minds. What does 'seared' mean in this context?

options.A: Burned or permanently fixed into memory.
options.B: Carefully analyzed and questioned by the patient.
options.C: Gradually forgotten over a long period of time.
options.D: Transformed into a more realistic set of goals.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在文中『seared』一詞用來形容患者對 AI 完美影像的執著，意指這些影像深深烙印在腦海中，難以抹去。這解釋了為何患者難以接受自然的術後結果。選項 B 與文意相反，患者反而沒有質疑這些影像；選項 C 與『seared』的強烈程度不符；選項 D 則完全曲解了該詞在文中的負面語境。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the influence of technology on beauty?

options.A: Plastic surgery is becoming obsolete due to the rise of advanced AI and weight-loss medications.
options.B: Patients should rely exclusively on AI tools to determine their ideal physical appearance.
options.C: The history of art proves that thinness has always been the only standard of beauty.
options.D: Modern technology creates unrealistic beauty expectations that require a critical and informed perspective.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章整體主旨探討了 AI 與醫療科技如何重塑大眾的自我形象，並強調這些技術創造了現實中難以達成的『完美』幻象。最後一段總結建議，大眾應對網路上看到的影像保持批判性思維，並理解其背後的軟體與醫療局限。選項 A 過於誇大；選項 B 與文章警告相悖；選項 C 錯誤，因為第四段提到歷史上曾以豐腴為美。
